item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2064-C "Ruth" is a 21 year old college student. Criminal records were expunged due to subject being a minor and first-time offender at the time.

I know what you were going for, but the way it's written makes it sound like she had a criminal record because she was a 21 year old college student.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m glad I wasn’t the only one who thought about that as well. Four robbers, lots of guns, different skills, increased pain tolerance? Yup. The resemblance is uncanny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally didn't like the undercurrent PAYDAY 2 reference.

In fact, it left enough of a bad taste of "this is all just riding on the concept of that game in a twisted, Foundation-y way" that, sorry, I'm -1'ing this one.

Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the idea is interesting, it lacks a… Well, thing? If the main anomaly is that Frank and Andy picked these four to be their burgling team (assuming Frank and Andy aren't under a similar effect themselves), it all feels like a painfully forced Payday Rule 63 attempt.

Why not show them security footage? Test if their behaviour changes if they are put together in twos and threes, or whether mind-wipey SCPs without lingering effects (cross-contamination, ho!) will negate the effect or not.

"Bring out… The telekill foil hat!" or somesuch, yanno?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Theme naming, I guess? Mr Pink, Mr Blue, Mr Orange, Mr Colour-Out-Of-Space and his lil' brother, Mr Ultraviolet.

item.title: Re: Chocolate
item.content:encoded: I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are all of them named after different candies for a reason?

item.title: Re: Chocolate
item.content:encoded: Baby ruth.

item.title: Re: Chocolate
item.content:encoded: Mars and Reese and Kit are easy, but how about Ruth?

item.title: Chocolate
item.content:encoded: Anybody else notice that their named after chocolate brands?…..+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's more than just an uncanny resemblance. Check out the author's statements at the top of the thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of payday 2. Group of four heisters, and tend to be more succesful in a full team, while tending to fail when split apart. Also, mayhem dressed in a suit… Its the name of the main menu music. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A reference to Mastermind's "inspire" skill would've been a nice touch, seeing as the person using it can just shout somebody to full health after numerous bullet wounds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The name "mayhem dressed in a suit" is the name of the very first song in the soundtrack of Payday 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Needs more content, the ending was abrupt and I didn't feel satisfied.

No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Answers are cheap

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As soon as I got to the part about the heists and shootouts I was thinking GTA V. I came here and learned it was Payday 2 (which I coincidentally just started playing!) instead, which I hadn't thought of. I liked it. My one minor complaint is that it didn't provide me with enough satisfying answers. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally enjoyed, and upvoted it. There were a few minor problems, especially in the interview portion, but all easily forgivable, it was quite the enjoyable SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Payday 2 fan here, I absolutely loved the article. Thank you for not butchering the payday gang and all those involved while making this SCP!

And uh, if this wasn't inspired by anything related to the Payday game, then ignore this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There were three of those you oaf XD.
That said, +1, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't they at least be Euclid? Safe seems to be a bit of an under reaction to homicidal women who threatened to slit a Foundation researcher's throat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty neat. I'm a little iffy on our ability to measure firearms ability, and Marlowe's dialogue in the second interview could use some tweaks, but overall I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EVIL CHARLIE's ANGELS!

Plus one!

With telepathy.

So, you know. Not Charlie's Angels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a group, the subjects are able to communicate complicated details and abstract concepts to one another non-verbally, and develop a higher tolerance for physical pain as a group.

PICK ONE!!!

Seriously. That^^ reads funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn't call it problem with pacing, so much as the story being convoluted. There really wasn't any reason for the Foundation to get involved here.

No vote.

EDIT: And this is called me not paying attention well after not eating for 25 hours. There was, indeed, a reason for them to pursue this case.

Upvoted, but the interviews could still use a bit of revision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not, actually, the dialogue outside of them together was also a bit awkward. And if it was intentional all over, I didn't see any evidence as to why. That said I don't think it's going to turn into a big problem. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If that's what other guise were talking about, then the 4 together's unnatural dialogue was intentional

EDIT: I've done some revamping of the dialogue and such, hoping to make it sound more natural and such. Didn't realize how stilted some of the stuff was until now, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, the unnatural dialogue is one of my favorite parts of this article. The four subjects appear to have been forced into the roles of bank robbers, and the unnatural way they speak accentuates the artificiality of their situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "pacing" how?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing these sentiments-although those issues didn't prevent me from upvoting anyway. I quite like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, but the thing suffers from some pacing problems and some of the dialogue is very unnatural. Have to downvote for now. I'll check back in on it later to see if it improves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject raises their bound hands

I know this was intended because clinical documents don't refer to gender, but "their" is plural and "subject" is singular. I believe it should read "its bound hands."

Upvoted, though. This is wonderfully wacky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
symptoms suspiciously similar to the whole Stuff ordeal

SPOILERSZ
– hide block

this was clearly inspired/based on "Payday 2"
